reasoning: The market estimate on May 15 was 18%. As of the May 2025 FOMC meeting, no rate cuts have occurred in 2025, keeping the target rate at 4.25-4.50%. Economic data and inflation concerns have shifted expectations heavily towards fewer cuts. In March, CME FedWatch showed exactly a 17.8% chance of a single 25 bps cut for the year, perfectly aligning with the recent Polymarket quote. With robust inflation concerns persisting post-May 23 (with Fed projections nudging upward regarding terminal rates), exactly 1 cut is wedged between the distinct possibilities of zero cuts (no net cuts) and two cuts, leaving its probability tightly anchored in the high teens.
